vocab.meaning: bắt đầu một tiến trình hoặc hoạt động nào đó
vocab.example: In my high school basketball team, I was often the one to get the ball rolling by organizing our extra practice sessions.

vocab.meaning: người biết phối hợp tốt với đồng đội
vocab.example: I've always strived to be a team player, prioritizing the group's success over my own personal statistics.

vocab.meaning: học cách làm một công việc hoặc hoạt động mới
vocab.example: Joining the local cricket club was daunting at first, but the senior players helped me learn the ropes quickly.

vocab.meaning: đánh bại đối thủ một cách dễ dàng
vocab.example: There was one season where our football team was so well-coordinated that we managed to blow the competition away.

vocab.word: keep one's eye on the ball
vocab.meaning: duy trì sự tập trung vào mục tiêu quan trọng
vocab.example: Being part of a competitive swimming relay taught me to keep my eye on the ball and ignore any distractions.

vocab.meaning: một môi trường cạnh tranh công bằng
vocab.example: I enjoyed school sports because the referees always ensured a level playing field for both sides.
Even when we were losing by a large margin, our coach encouraged us to always give it our best shot until the final whistle.

vocab.word: the ball is in someone's court
vocab.meaning: đến lượt ai đó chịu trách nhiệm đưa ra quyết định hoặc hành động
vocab.example: Once the captain sets the strategy, the ball is in our court to execute the plays effectively on the field.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực nhiều hơn mức được yêu cầu
vocab.example: To make the varsity team, I had to go the extra mile by training in the gym long after the school day ended.

vocab.meaning: chiến thắng một cách cực kỳ dễ dàng
vocab.example: Because we practiced our set pieces daily, we were able to win the regional finals hands down.
